purpose: Prescriptive companion to this repo's data-analysis bulletins -- turns the import/export chapter rankings, country-concentration findings, and PLI-coverage checks into explicit, tiered sector and policy recommendations, cross-checked against NITI Aayog's Trade Watch Quarterly framework.

validation_sources.source: NITI Aayog Trade Watch Quarterly, Q1 FY2024-25 edition (Apr-Jun 2024)
validation_sources.note: User-supplied PDF; used for methodology/structure/RCA-TII framing, NOT for exact FY2025-26 figure validation since it predates this repo's core claims by 1-2 years.

validation_sources.source: NITI Aayog Trade Watch Quarterly, 8th edition, Q4 FY2025-26
DGCI&S Monthly Bulletin on Foreign Trade Statistics, March 2024
validation_sources.note: User-supplied PDF; confirms no explicit blanket FX-conversion rule is published, and that DGCI&S data is provisional/dynamically revised -- explains TIA Portal vs TRADESTAT/PIB vintage differences.
User-supplied PDF; source of the official 13-sector HS-2 taxonomy (Annexure 1) used below, and evidence that DGCI&S's own raw data carries period-specific (not blanket-annual) USD/INR conversion.

import_substitution_tiers.label: Act now -- no scheme, genuine gap

chapters.name: Fertilisers
chapters.existing_scheme: National Investment Policy for Urea-2026, approved 15 Jul 2026 -- 3 days before this repo's original import-dependency bulletin

import_substitution_tiers.label: Different policy lever needed, not PLI-shaped

chapters.name: Mineral fuels & oils
chapters.note: Needs energy-transition policy (EVs, domestic E&P, biofuels), not a production incentive.

chapters.name: Pearls, gems & jewellery
chapters.note: Largely the intentional duty-free-for-re-export channel (FTP Chapter 4) funding India's own gems export industry.

export_growth_tiers.label: Fix the PLI mismatch
export_growth_tiers.recommendation: Broaden PLI Textiles' scope to cover cotton-based apparel, or create a parallel cotton-apparel-specific incentive.

export_growth_tiers.label: Double down, diversify buyers
export_growth_tiers.recommendation: Reduce USA over-concentration (11 of 12 growth chapters, #1/#2 in 9) by deepening UAE/EU/ASEAN as deliberate diversification targets.

export_growth_tiers.label: Open opportunity, no scheme
export_growth_tiers.recommendation: Needs export infrastructure / phytosanitary certification capacity / trade-agreement market access, not manufacturing PLI.

country_relationship_tiers.label: De-risk
country_relationship_tiers.note: PLI Electronics/ISM 2.0 coverage already strong -- this is an execution/scaling problem (~62% electronic-component dependency 6 years into PLI), not a missing-policy problem.

country_relationship_tiers.label: Diversify
country_relationship_tiers.note: Mirror risk to China on imports -- more concentrated by breadth and depth. Treat UAE/EU/ASEAN as deliberate diversification targets.

country_relationship_tiers.label: Monitor, not a policy gap
country_relationship_tiers.note: Russia's rise is market economics + sanctions-workaround payment structure, not substitutable dependency. UAE/Switzerland deficits are structural bullion/re-export flows that deliberately stay open to fund India's own gems exports.

cbam_export_risk.note: EU CBAM's initial scope covers iron/steel, aluminium, cement, fertilizer, hydrogen, electricity -- a demand-side/market-access risk distinct from the production-capacity focus of Specialty Steel PLI (Rs 44,106cr + Rs 11,887cr). Decarbonizing production, not just scaling it, is the policy lever this risk calls for.

declining_export_sector.name: Pearls, gems & jewellery
declining_export_sector.note: Independently corroborated by NITI Aayog Trade Watch's own finding that several labour-intensive export sectors (natural/cultured pearls, lac/gums/resins, man-made filaments, leather articles) have lost global market share since 2015 -- same chapter, same direction, found via a completely different method (TRADESTAT chapter trend vs. NITI Aayog market-share analysis).

methodology_gaps_vs_niti_aayog.rca_not_computed: NITI Aayog computes Revealed Comparative Advantage per commodity (43 commodities clear RCA>1, 40% of global imports, 68.2% of India's export portfolio) using global commodity-share data (UN Comtrade/WTO) this repo hasn't collected. This repo's rankings are dollar-value and PLI-coverage based, not comparative-advantage based.
methodology_gaps_vs_niti_aayog.tii_not_computed: NITI Aayog computes a Trade Intensity Index per partner country (>1 with 84 countries, 67% of India's exports) using GDP-weighted bilateral trade shares. This repo's country rankings are combined-exposure-in-dollars based, a related but methodologically distinct concentration measure.
